protection-from-energy.canonicalEvidence: Resistance to one damage type of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.
protection-from-energy.structuredState: This is a defensive target effect, not an environmental change.
protection-from-energy.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no environmental effect.
protection-from-energy.issue: The detector matched elemental damage words, but this row is not an environmental mechanic.
protection-from-energy.recommendedTemplateChange: No environmental field required for this row.

protection-from-energy.jsonState: Runtime targeting uses allies and has no first-class willing target filter.
protection-from-energy.issue: The spell requires a willing creature target, but runtime flattens willingness into ally targeting.
protection-from-energy.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by Target Willingness propagation: structured markdown now records Target Willingness for every spell and required for this direct willing-target spell.
protection-from-energy.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by Target Willingness propagation: targeting.filter.willing is required for this spell and not_applicable elsewhere.
protection-from-energy.reviewerNote: Closed because the new structured/runtime willingness field fully covers this direct willing-target mechanic.

remove-curse.canonicalEvidence: At your touch, all curses affecting one creature or object end. If the object is a cursed magic item, its curse remains, but the spell breaks its owner Attunement to the object so it can be removed or discarded.
remove-curse.structuredState: Valid Targets now list creatures and objects; no curse-ending or attunement-break fields.
remove-curse.jsonState: Runtime targeting now allows creatures and objects, but the curse/attunement mechanics are prose-only.
remove-curse.issue: Remove Curse ends ordinary curses and has a different cursed-magic-item branch that breaks attunement without ending the item curse.
remove-curse.recommendedTemplateChange: Add curseRemoval and cursedMagicItem attunement-break fields.
remove-curse.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime curseEffect data for end curses on creature/object and break owner attunement for cursed magic items.

remove-curse.structuredState: Valid Targets now list creatures and objects.
remove-curse.jsonState: Runtime validTargets now include creatures and objects.
remove-curse.issue: The creature-or-object target eligibility is represented.
remove-curse.recommendedTemplateChange: No target eligibility change required for this row.
remove-curse.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding objects to runtime validTargets and normalizing structured Valid Targets to creatures, objects.

revivify.canonicalEvidence: You touch a creature that has died within the last minute. This spell cannot revive a creature that has died of old age.
revivify.structuredState: Valid Targets: dead_creature; no death age/timing or old-age exclusion fields.
revivify.jsonState: Runtime targeting only allows creatures and has no dead/recent-death filters.
revivify.issue: Revivify requires a dead creature that died within the last minute and excludes old age deaths.
revivify.recommendedTemplateChange: Add death-state target filters for max time since death and old-age exclusion.
revivify.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime targetFilter dead=true, diedWithin 1 minute, excludeDeathCause old_age.

revivify.canonicalEvidence: That creature revives with 1 Hit Point.
revivify.structuredState: Effect Type: HEALING but no revive/restoration field or fixed 1 HP value.
revivify.jsonState: Runtime JSON is prose UTILITY with no HEALING/restoration effect.
revivify.issue: Revivify restores a dead creature to life with exactly 1 HP, which is not ordinary healing data.
revivify.recommendedTemplateChange: Add revive/restoration fields with fixed resulting HP.
revivify.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime REVIVE or restoration effect setting revived target HP to 1.

revivify.canonicalEvidence: A diamond worth 300+ GP, which the spell consumes.
revivify.jsonState: Runtime components preserve materialCost 300 and isConsumed true.
revivify.issue: The consumed 300 GP diamond component is represented.

revivify.canonicalEvidence: Nor does it restore any missing body parts.
revivify.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no body-part limitation data.
revivify.issue: Revivify revives without restoring missing body parts; that limitation is not represented.

sending.canonicalEvidence: You send a short message of 25 words or fewer. The target hears the message in its mind and can answer in a like manner immediately. The spell enables targets to understand the meaning of your message.
sending.structuredState: Effect Type: UTILITY; no message length, mental delivery, reply, or comprehension fields.
sending.jsonState: Runtime JSON has one prose utility effect.
sending.issue: Sending has a rich communication payload that is not typed.
sending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add communication fields for word limit, target qualification, mental delivery, immediate reply, and meaning comprehension.
sending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime communication data for wordLimit 25, mentalMessage, immediateReply, and comprehendMeaning.

sending.canonicalEvidence: You can send the message across any distance and even to other planes of existence, but if the target is on a different plane than you, there is a 5 percent chance that the message does not arrive.
sending.structuredState: Range Type: unlimited; no cross-plane failure chance field.
sending.jsonState: Runtime range is 0 and has no planar delivery data.
sending.issue: Sending supports unlimited/cross-planar messaging with a 5 percent cross-plane failure chance.
sending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add cross-planar communication and failure chance fields.
sending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime deliveryRange anyDistance, crossPlaneAllowed true, crossPlaneFailureChance 5 percent.
